I recently attended Microsoft 's event on “Architecting the Agentic AI Stack”: and it genuinely shifted how I think about building AI systems at scale.
...
key takeaways that stood out:
...
🔹 Purpose-driven AI Agents are the next layer
...
We’re moving beyond “AI that assists” to AI agents that can be assigned jobs. These agents operate with defined goals, constraints, and accountability, making them far more production-ready.
...
🔹 Coding isn’t the bottleneck anymore, review is
...
With the immense rise of AI-assisted coding, velocity has skyrocketed. But now, the constraint is shifting toward validation, governance, and review pipelines. The conversation around responsible AI integration is becoming more critical than ever.
...
🔹 Decoupling models from applications
...
One powerful idea discussed was separating model dependency from application logic.
...
With Microsoft Foundry’s model router, applications can dynamically route requests across models, enabling flexibility, cost efficiency, and resilience without tight coupling.
...
🔹 Bigger context windows ≠ always better
...
An important nuance: increasing context size isn’t automatically a win. In some scenarios, SLMs (Small Language Models) can outperform LLMs when predictability, latency, or cost control matters more than generative breadth.
...
🔹 Rise of AI agents in SRE, Security & DevOps
...
We’re entering an era where multi-agent systems collaborate - monitoring systems, responding to incidents, auditing security events, optimizing deployments.
...
The concept of an “Agent HQ”: orchestrating multiple specialized agents, feels less futuristic and more inevitable.
...
Overall, the session reinforced one thing:
...
We’re no longer just building AI features.
...
We’re architecting AI ecosystems.
...
Grateful to the presenters and speakers Sandeep Alur , Jaideep F John , Karthik Rameshkumar , Arunangshu Basu , Somnath Banerjee for the deep insights and practical framing of where the agentic AI stack is heading. #AI #AgenticAI #Microsoft #DevOps #SRE #Architecture #LLM #SLM #AIEngineering #MultiAgentSystems #MicrosoftFoundry
